52	**Rev 4 design pivot (per user direction 2026-05-03):**
53	"Workers should always be autonomous. If they have a server to talk to,
54	they send results there. They should be able to do their own work and
55	store their logs and artifacts locally without regard for the server.
56	The server is strictly a value-added coordinator."
57	
58	This shifts the decision from Proposal A (server orchestrates; --local has
59	in-process API impl) to a Proposal B-shaped design: workers are always
60	autonomous against the bead store; server is an optional reporting target
61	that provides centralized observability, cross-worker visibility, and UI
62	surfaces — but is NOT required for correct operation. `--local` becomes
63	"force standalone mode; don't try to reach a server" — the same code
64	path that workers take when no server is reachable.

136	
137	## Context
138	
139	Operators have observed a structural failure mode: when `ddx-server`
140	restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the
141	orchestrator. Today there are two execution paths that diverge in lifecycle,
142	configuration plumbing, and observability:
143	
144	- **`--local` path.** `ddx work` (a.k.a. `ddx agent execute-loop`) runs an
145	  in-process drain loop that talks to the bead store directly
146	  (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`).
147	- **Server-spawned path.** The server forks a worker process, hand-marshals
148	  an `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` over an internal contract, and tracks the PID
149	  in `.ddx/workers/`. The worker reaches back into the bead store directly,
150	  not through the server.
151	
152	Three open beads diagnose the symptom-space:
153	
154	- `ddx-29058e2a` — five flags silently dropped on the server path because
155	  the spec is hand-maintained as parallel structs across six layers.
156	- `ddx-5cb6e6cd` — refactor epic that splits `execute_bead_loop` into
157	  `run/`, `try/`, `work/` packages, exposing an implicit state machine
158	  inside a 700-line function.
159	- `ddx-dc157075` — workers exit prematurely on an empty queue because
160	  `--poll-interval=0` is the default and the per-Run `attempted` map is
161	  scoped to one drain pass.
162	
163	`ddx-4c51d33e` (closed) demonstrated the related security problem: any
164	worker that touches the bead store directly has to reimplement project
165	scoping; the GraphQL resolver got that wrong for `DocumentByPath`.
166	
167	The common cause: **the worker is coupled to local in-process state and
168	the server has no first-class concept of a worker beyond a PID it forked.**
169	Restart loses the in-flight context because the context lives nowhere
170	addressable. Each fix to one of the three beads above fixes a leaf without
171	addressing the trunk.
172	
173	This ADR exists because the next round of refactors (the `try/` and `work/`
174	packages, the `ExecuteLoopSpec` unification, the stay-alive fix) will all
175	re-cement the current bipartite design unless the architectural shift is
176	agreed first.
177	
178	## Decision
179	
180	**Workers are always autonomous. There is no "--local mode" — the
181	worker has one mode (autonomous) and detects a server continuously. If
182	a server is reachable, the worker emits events and results to it
183	best-effort. If no server is reachable (none running, partition, never
184	configured), the worker proceeds with its work and tries again later.
185	The bead store is the source of truth; the server is an optional
186	observability + coordination target.**
187	
188	Concretely:
189	
190	1. A worker process (`ddx work`) reads the bead store directly, picks
191	   the next eligible bead per the current picker logic (priority asc,
192	   FIFO within tier), claims it atomically, executes via `try.Attempt`,
193	   writes evidence locally, and reports the result by writing the bead's
194	   event log + closing the bead via the store.
195	2. **All of #1 happens regardless of whether a server is running.**
196	   This is the existing autonomous behavior; the worker has no
197	   dependency on a server for correctness.
198	3. **In parallel**, a small "server probe" goroutine inside the worker
199	   periodically (every N seconds, default 30) checks
200	   `~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr` (XDG-compliant, matches current code) for a reachable server. State transitions:
201	   - **NotConnected → Connected:** server appeared; worker POSTs
202	     `register` with a thin identity envelope (project root, harness,
203	     executor pid/host); on success, worker stores the `worker_id` and
204	     starts mirroring events to the server best-effort.
205	   - **Connected → Connected:** server still reachable; continue
206	     mirroring.
207	   - **Connected → NotConnected:** server unreachable (crash,
208	     partition, stopped); worker logs the transition, stops trying to
209	     mirror events, retains its bead-store-local event log, continues
210	     working.
211	   - **NotConnected → NotConnected:** still no server; nothing to do.
212	4. When in Connected state, the worker mirrors bead events + final
213	   results to `/api/workers/<id>/event` and the bead-close result to
214	   the same endpoint best-effort. Failures (server crash mid-POST,
215	   partition, 5xx) are logged locally and ignored — the bead's local
216	   event log is authoritative.
217	5. The server's view (which workers exist, which beads are running,
218	   recent events) is **derived from worker reports** — eventually
219	   consistent, NOT authoritative. The server's value is centralized
220	   observability, cross-worker dashboards, operator UI, and
221	   server-initiated cancel (which writes a marker to the bead store
222	   that workers honor on next iteration).
223	6. Server restart drops the in-memory derived view; on next probe cycle
224	   the worker re-registers (or notices the server is gone). No
225	   in-flight work is at risk because workers don't depend on the
226	   server.
227	
228	**`--local` flag is removed.** Today's `--local` semantics are the
229	default behavior; there's nothing to opt into. Operators who pass
230	`--local` see a deprecation warning + the flag is a no-op (no
231	"force standalone" behavior — the worker probes for a server normally;
232	operators wanting to suppress the probe can stop the server). After one
233	alpha release with the deprecation warning, the flag is deleted. The
234	breaking-changes section documents this.
235	
236	This is **Proposal B-shaped** (server is passive observer) with one
237	addition: the server can write back to the bead store to influence
238	worker decisions (e.g., cancel a bead by setting an extra field; set
239	priority overrides). Workers see those changes on next bead-store read.
240	
241	### Why autonomous-default workers
242	
243	- **Workers don't depend on the server for correctness.** Any server
244	  restart, partition, crash, or just "no server running" is a no-op for
245	  the worker's primary job (executing beads).
246	- **Same code path for every invocation.** `ddx work` and `ddx work
247	  --local` differ only in whether they bother trying to reach a server.
248	  No in-process API server, no Transport interface, no test-mode
249	  fixture — the existing unit-test setup (no server) is the autonomous
250	  case.
251	- **Server is opt-in observability.** Operators who want centralized
252	  dashboards run a server; operators who don't, don't. Either is
253	  correct.
254	- **No claim coordination races.** The bead store's atomic claim is the
255	  only claim primitive. Two workers contending for the same bead use
256	  the same store-level CAS that exists today; there is no parallel
257	  server-side claim table to race against.
258	- **Project boundaries enforced by the existing per-project bead store.**
259	  Each `.ddx/beads.jsonl` is a project; workers operate against one
260	  project's store; the cross-project leak class (`ddx-4c51d33e`) is
261	  prevented at the worker level because workers never read another
262	  project's store.
263	- **`ExecuteLoopSpec` from `ddx-29058e2a` still applies** — but as a
264	  worker-side struct unification (cobra → in-process flow), not a wire
265	  format. The drop class is closed by the same single-struct
266	  discipline; it just doesn't need to cross a network boundary.
267	
268	### Threat model (limits of session-token project binding)
269	
270	Session tokens prevent **honest worker mixup** (a misconfigured worker for
271	project A cannot accidentally claim a bead from project B). They are NOT a
272	defense against a **local malicious process**: `requireTrusted` accepts any
273	loopback connection without identity proof, so any process on the same host
274	can register as any project. The session-token model is project-scoping for
275	legitimate workers, not authentication of worker identity. Future work to
276	strengthen this (e.g., per-worker keypairs, ts-net WhoIs binding for
277	loopback registrations) is out of scope for v1 and tracked separately.
278	
279	### Picker (worker-side, status quo with diagnostic events)
280	
281	The bead picker stays where it lives today: in the worker, against the
282	bead store. The fix at commit `80f51574` added `picker.priority_skip`
283	and `picker.claim_race` diagnostic events; both are preserved. Picker
284	rules (unchanged from current code):
285	
286	1. Read eligible beads via `Store.ReadyExecution()` (excludes superseded,
287	   not-execution-eligible, in retry-cooldown).
288	2. Sort by priority ascending (P0 first), then by `updated_at` ascending
289	   (FIFO within tier).
290	3. If `--label-filter` is set, intersect with bead labels.
291	4. Skip beads in the per-Run `attempted` map (set after each unsuccessful
292	   claim attempt; reset on falling through to "no candidate" per the
293	   stay-alive fix at commit `41cb762e`).
294	5. Claim atomically via `Store.Claim()`; on CAS loss, emit
295	   `picker.claim_race` and continue to next eligible.
296	
297	Two workers contending for the same top bead use the bead store's
298	existing atomic claim — the same primitive that has worked correctly
299	for as long as DDx has had a bead store. There is no parallel server-side
300	claim table to race against.
301	
302	When the server is reachable, the worker mirrors the
303	`picker.priority_skip` and `picker.claim_race` events to the server's
304	ingestion endpoint so operators can see them in the UI. When no server,
305	the events still land in the bead's local event stream.
306	
307	### Why not centralized server-side picker (the rev 1-3 design)
308	
309	- Workers don't need it for correctness — the bead store's atomic claim
310	  is sufficient. Adding a server-side claim table creates a parallel
311	  source of truth that has to be reconciled.
312	- Server-side picker requires workers to depend on the server, which
313	  contradicts the autonomous-default decision.
314	- Diagnostic events from commit `80f51574` already make starvation
315	  observable; centralization didn't add visibility we lacked.
316	
317	### Why not Proposal A (server orchestrates, in-process API for --local)
318	
319	(Earlier revisions chose this; rev 4 reverses.)
320	
321	- The in-process API server for `--local` was complexity in service of a
322	  uniformity that the user explicitly doesn't need. `--local` working
323	  *without* a server is the natural and operator-expected behavior.
324	- "Server is the source of truth for runtime state" requires server
325	  restart recovery, claim reconciliation, partition handling, and a
326	  long-poll back-pressure regime — none of which are needed when the
327	  bead store is the source of truth.
328	- Workers being long-lived clients of a server requires the server to be
329	  available for the worker to function, which the user explicitly
330	  rejected.
331	
332	## Worker-server interface (best-effort reporting)
333	
334	The worker-server boundary is small. There is no HTTP API for claim
335	coordination, no long-poll, no heartbeat lease, no in-process server.
336	There are exactly two endpoints, both used best-effort by workers and
337	both backed by `requireTrusted` (loopback or ts-net per ADR-006):
338	
339	### POST /api/workers/register
340	
341	Worker-side: called by the server-probe goroutine on every
342	NotConnected → Connected transition (NOT just at startup). On
343	re-connect after server crash, the worker re-registers and gets a new
344	`worker_id`. Discovery uses
345	`~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr` (XDG-compliant, matches current code)
346	discovery. Body is a small identity envelope:
347	
348	```json
349	{
350	  "project_root": "/abs/path",
351	  "harness": "claude",
352	  "model": "",
353	  "executor_pid": 12345,
354	  "executor_host": "host.local",
355	  "started_at": "2026-05-03T03:00:00Z"
356	}
357	```
358	
359	Response:
360	
361	```json
362	{ "worker_id": "wkr-9f3a..." }
363	```
364	
365	The worker uses `worker_id` as a correlation key in subsequent event
366	reports. If registration fails (no server, network error, 5xx), the
367	worker proceeds without a `worker_id` and skips event mirroring. No
368	session token, no heartbeat, no claim lease — none of those concepts
369	exist in the worker-server protocol because the worker doesn't depend
370	on the server.
371	
372	### POST /api/workers/<id>/event
373	
374	Worker-side: called best-effort whenever the worker would write an event
375	to a bead's local event log. Body mirrors the bead event:
376	
377	```json
378	{
379	  "bead_id": "ddx-076147ee",
380	  "attempt_id": "20260503T021424-dac100b6",
381	  "kind": "attempt.started" | "picker.priority_skip" | "loop.idle" | "result" | "...",
382	  "body": { "...": "..." }
383	}
384	```
385	
386	Server appends to its derived view (in-memory + optionally backed by
387	its own `.ddx/server/events.jsonl`). Response: 204. Worker's local
388	event log is the authoritative copy; server-side is for observability.
389	
390	If the POST fails for any reason, the worker logs the failure locally
391	and continues. Lost events are tolerable because the bead's local event
392	stream has the full record; operators wanting to backfill can replay
393	from the bead store.
394	
395	### What the server can do (the value-add)
396	
397	Once the server has a derived view of who's reporting:
398	
399	- **Centralized observability**: which workers exist, what they're doing
400	  right now, recent picker decisions, recent dropped commits — all
401	  surfaced via existing GraphQL and the workers panel.
402	- **Cross-worker correlation**: see all workers across all projects in
403	  one place; useful for multi-project hosts.
404	- **Operator UI**: queue beads, cancel beads (via writing markers to the
405	  bead store that workers honor on next iteration), view bead history.
406	- **Server-initiated cancel**: not a direct API call to the worker;
407	  instead, server writes `extra.cancel-requested: true` to the bead;
408	  worker observes on next iteration of its loop and aborts the attempt
409	  at the next safe point.
410	
411	### What the server explicitly does NOT do
412	
413	- It does not assign beads to workers (workers pick from the bead store
414	  themselves).
415	- It does not hold authoritative claim state (the bead store does).
416	- It does not gate worker startup or operation (workers run without it).
417	- It does not hold session tokens or enforce per-worker auth (a future
418	  hardening; v1 trusts the requireTrusted boundary).
419	- It does not need to survive worker death gracefully (workers are
420	  independent; server's derived view is replayable from the bead
421	  stores at any time).
422	
423	### Server crash / restart behavior
424	
425	Server crashes: workers' next event POST fails; workers log + continue.
426	Server restarts: workers' next event POST succeeds (or 410 if the
427	server didn't recover the worker_id, in which case the worker
428	re-registers and resumes). No in-flight work is at risk.
429	
430	### Probe + freshness state model
431	
432	Smaller than rev 3's claim/heartbeat model but still needs a single
433	table. The server-probe goroutine:
434	
435	| Event | Threshold | Action |
436	|---|---|---|
437	| Worker process start | immediate (0s) | Probe `~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr`; on success, register |
438	| Probe interval (steady state) | 30s default; 10s min; 5min max; jittered ±20% | Re-check reachability; emit register if NotConnected→Connected; nothing if Connected→Connected |
439	| Connected POST fails (timeout, conn refused, 5xx) | immediate | Worker enters NotConnected; logs locally; continues working |
440	| 5 consecutive probe failures | ~2.5 min default | Worker reduces probe rate to 5min (backoff); resets to 30s on next success |
441	| Server replies 410 unknown_worker | immediate | Worker re-registers within same probe cycle |
442	| Worker process exit (graceful) | immediate | Best-effort POST disconnect; not required for correctness |
443	
444	**Freshness indicators** (server-side, surfaced in the workers panel UI):
445	
446	- `worker_state` ∈ {connected, stale, disconnected}: connected = event
447	  in last 2× probe interval; stale = 2-10× probe interval; disconnected
448	  = > 10× probe interval (worker presumed dead)
449	- `last_event_at`: timestamp of most recent event from this worker
450	- `mirror_failures_count`: total POST failures since worker_id was issued
451	  (operator can see if a worker is healthy but lossy)
452	
453	### Disconnected-work backfill
454	
455	A worker that started while no server was running may have claimed and
456	finished beads before reaching Connected. To prevent the operator's UI
457	from missing those events:
458	
459	- The worker maintains an in-memory ring buffer of the last N=200
460	  events it emitted while NotConnected (kind, body, bead_id,
461	  attempt_id, timestamp).
462	- On NotConnected → Connected transition, the worker POSTs a
463	  `/api/workers/<id>/backfill` request with the buffered events. Server
464	  acknowledges with 204; worker clears the buffer.
465	- If the buffer overflows (worker generated >200 events while
466	  disconnected — possible during a long outage or many short attempts),
467	  oldest events are dropped silently. The bead's local event log
468	  remains complete; only the server's derived view is incomplete. UI
469	  marks the worker as `had_dropped_backfill: true` so operators know to
470	  consult bead-local logs.
471	- Backfill is best-effort: a 5xx during backfill leaves the buffer
472	  intact; worker tries again on next NotConnected → Connected.
473	
474	### Cancel SLA
475	
476	Operator-initiated cancel writes `extra.cancel-requested: true` to the
477	bead via the server's `/api/beads/<id>/cancel` endpoint (or directly
478	via the bead store). The worker honors at the next safe point:
479	
480	- **Mid-attempt poll** (default every 10s during long attempts): the
481	  worker re-reads the bead's `extra` map. If `cancel-requested: true`
482	  appears, the worker aborts the next LLM turn / next git operation
483	  boundary and reports `preserved_for_review` with reason `operator_cancel`.
484	- **Worst-case latency**: 10s (mid-attempt poll interval) plus the
485	  current LLM turn duration (typically 5-30s). Operators expecting
486	  faster cancel use OS signals.
487	- **Idempotency**: a bead with `cancel-requested: true` already worked
488	  is silently consumed (worker writes `cancel-honored: true` next to
489	  it); a worker starting work on a bead with `cancel-requested: true`
490	  immediately reports `preserved_for_review` and skips the attempt.
491	
492	## Compatibility analysis
493	
494	### Migrates cleanly (almost everything)
495	
496	- **Existing autonomous worker behavior.** This is the baseline rev 4
497	  preserves. Today's `ddx work` and `ddx work --local` already work
498	  against the bead store; rev 4 is additive (best-effort server
499	  reporting on top of unchanged behavior).
500	- **Bead event log shape.** Worker events go to the bead's local event
501	  log (today's behavior); the server-side ingestion endpoint receives a
502	  copy with the same shape. No reader changes.
503	- **Evidence layout.** `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` unchanged.
504	- **`try.Attempt` semantics.** Unchanged. The worker calls into `try`
505	  exactly as today.
506	- **`requireTrusted` boundary.** Reused for the two new endpoints; no
507	  new auth plane.
508	- **Existing per-Run `attempted` map and stay-alive defaults.** Preserved
509	  from commit `41cb762e`. The autonomous picker is the picker.
510	
511	### Breaking changes for operators
512	
513	- **None for the autonomous path.** `ddx work` and `ddx work --local`
514	  behave identically to today (with the addition of best-effort server
515	  reporting when a server is reachable).
516	- **Some legacy server-spawn integration tests will need updating.** The
517	  current server-spawn path that hand-marshals `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec`
518	  over an internal contract is replaced by the server `exec`'ing
519	  `ddx work` like an operator would. Tests asserting specific spawn
520	  args will need to assert the new exec form. (No operator-visible
521	  behavior change here — operators don't call the legacy endpoint
522	  directly.)
523	- **Per-user direction**: the previous `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` path
524	  through the server is removed; no compat shim and no data migration
525	  for legacy `.ddx/workers/` records (delete history if needed).
526	
527	### Tests that need updating (smaller list than rev 1-3)
528	
529	- `cli/internal/server/workers_test.go` — exec-spawn tests: assert the
530	  new exec form (`ddx work` invocation), not the JSON request/response
531	  envelope.
532	- Integration tests asserting specific `.ddx/workers/` on-disk format
533	  may need adjustment if rev 4 changes that layout. Currently rev 4 does
534	  NOT modify `.ddx/workers/` shape; workers continue to write spec.json
535	  + status.json there for `ddx agent doctor` consumption.
536	- New tests for the two endpoints: `TestWorkerRegister_HappyPath`,
537	  `TestWorkerRegister_ServerDown_WorkerProceeds`,
538	  `TestWorkerEvent_Mirrored_BestEffort`,
539	  `TestWorkerEvent_ServerCrash_WorkerLogs_Continues`.
540	
541	The tests-that-break list is dramatically smaller than rev 1-3 because
542	the worker state machine, bead store interaction, and evidence layout
543	are all unchanged. Only the new server-side ingestion endpoint and its
544	two tests are net-new.

546	## Sequencing relative to in-flight work
547	
548	Rev 4 dramatically simplifies sequencing. The architecture is additive:
549	the worker keeps working as it does today; server-side reporting is new
550	machinery that sits alongside. There is no "freeze C5/C7/C9 until X"
551	rule because nothing about the worker state machine changes.
552	
553	- **`ddx-29058e2a` (ExecuteLoopSpec unification)** — independent. Rev 4
554	  does NOT make ExecuteLoopSpec a wire format. The bead remains valuable
555	  as a worker-side struct unification (cobra → in-process flow) and
556	  should land independently.
557	- **`ddx-5cb6e6cd` C5 (no_changes adjudication)** — NOT FROZEN. Rev 5
558	  doesn't reshape no_changes generation; mirroring stays an observer.
559	- **`ddx-5cb6e6cd` C7 (uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps)** — **COORDINATE.** Direct conflict: rev 5's worker keeps the per-Run `attempted` map (preserved from commit `41cb762e`); C7 wants to delete it. Resolution: C7 lands BEFORE rev 5 implementation work, OR rev 5 implementation work coordinates with C7's replacement (Guard-derived exclusion). Until coordinated, C7 stays parked.
560	- **`ddx-5cb6e6cd` C9 (StopCondition + cost-cap)** — NOT FROZEN. The autonomous worker keeps the stay-alive defaults; C9's StopCondition becomes an internal worker construct, server sees terminal state via the result event.
561	- **`ddx-dc157075` (stay-alive at commit `41cb762e`)** — already shipped
562	  and preserved. Worker's autonomous behavior plus 30s poll-interval
563	  default carry forward unchanged.
564	- **`ddx-4c51d33e` (cross-project leak at commit `33b97f25`)** — already
565	  shipped. Rev 4 prevents recurrence at the worker level (one worker

584	## Implementation roadmap
585	
586	Rev 5 expands from rev 4's 5 beads to 9, after codex flagged missing
587	UI/doctor/FEAT-006/server-spawn-migration/backfill scope.
588	
589	1. **server: ingestion endpoints** — implement `POST /api/workers/register` + `POST /api/workers/<id>/event` + `POST /api/workers/<id>/backfill`; in-memory derived view backed by append-only `.ddx/server/worker-events.jsonl`; freshness state machine (connected/stale/disconnected); requireTrusted boundary. ~300 LOC.
590	
591	2. **worker: server-probe goroutine + best-effort event mirror** — periodic reachability check on `~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr` (XDG-compliant, matches current code) with jittered 30s interval, immediate first probe, exponential backoff on consecutive failures; transitions emit register/disconnect; in-memory ring buffer for backfill (200-event cap); event POSTs are best-effort. ~250 LOC.
592	
593	3. **server: operator-cancel** — `/api/beads/<id>/cancel` writes `extra.cancel-requested: true`; worker mid-attempt poll (10s default) checks bead `extra` and aborts at next safe point with `preserved_for_review` reason `operator_cancel`; idempotency via `cancel-honored: true`. ~150 LOC.
594	
595	4. **CLI: deprecate `--local`** — flag becomes no-op with deprecation warning; update CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, getting-started, and the cobra help text. Existing tests asserting `--local`-specific behavior get rewritten or deleted. (Codex notes the sweep is larger than 50 LOC: `cli/cmd/work_test.go`, `agent_execute_loop_test.go`, `zero_config_work_test.go`, `skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, demos.) ~150 LOC + doc edits.
596	
597	5. **server: derived-view GraphQL + UI workers panel** — schema fields: `workers { id, project, harness, state, last_event_at, mirror_failures_count, had_dropped_backfill, current_bead, current_attempt }`; UI surfaces freshness indicators + duplicate-worker display + the "trusted-peer reported, not authoritative" labeling. Existing workers panel migrated. ~400 LOC frontend + ~100 LOC backend.
598	
599	6. **`ddx agent doctor` migration** — read worker state from server's runtime registry when available; fall back to `.ddx/workers/` on-disk files when no server. The on-disk format stays as the fallback-source-of-truth for one alpha release lag, then deprecated. ~150 LOC.
600	
601	7. **server-spawn path migration** — `cli/internal/server/workers.go` and `handleStartExecuteLoopWorker` switch from hand-marshalled spec to `exec ddx work` with env-vars. The legacy spec serialization deletes when this lands. ~200 LOC + test updates.
602	
603	8. **FEAT-006 amendment** — replace the rev 3 worker-contract section with rev 5's autonomous-default + best-effort-mirror description. Drop session-token/heartbeat/`next-bead`/result/disconnect contract; add register/event/backfill/cancel + freshness state. ~200 lines docs.
604	
605	9. **acceptance + soak** — uses `ddx-50da9674` clean fixture repo. Multi-worker drain WITH server; restart server mid-flight; verify worker continues + reconnects + backfills. Then drain with NO server; verify identical behavior. Operator-cancel test (write marker, observe `preserved_for_review` with `operator_cancel` reason within SLA). Soak: ≥1 week of normal operator use. Final bead.
606	
607	Plus one COORDINATION bead, NOT new work but a parking-lot for the
608	C7 conflict:
609	
610	10. **coordinate: C7 (Guard contract, delete attempted map) with rev 5 worker** — either C7 lands first and rev 5 implementation uses Guard-derived exclusion, OR C7 stays parked until rev 5's worker code defines its replacement for the per-Run attempted map. Decision documented; no LOC.
611	
612	Total estimated effort: 2-4 weeks (vs 1-2 weeks for rev 4's understated 5-bead roadmap, vs 8-10 weeks for rev 3's design).
613	
614	## Consequences
615	
616	- **Workers don't depend on the server.** The headline operator
617	  requirement (server restart preserves in-flight work) is satisfied
618	  by construction: workers don't notice server restarts as anything
619	  more than transient mirror failures.
620	- **No `--local` flag.** Removed from the CLI surface. Behavior of
621	  today's `--local` becomes the always-on default. Backward compat
622	  preserved for one release via a no-op flag with deprecation warning.
623	- **Server is opt-in observability.** Operators who don't run a server
624	  see no behavior change from today. Operators who do see
625	  centralized event aggregation, cross-worker visibility, and an
626	  operator UI. Both are correct configurations.
627	- **`ExecuteLoopSpec` unification still happens.** Per `ddx-29058e2a` —
628	  the spec is a worker-side struct, not a wire format. The drop class
629	  is closed by single-struct discipline; it just doesn't need to cross
630	  a network boundary.
631	- **No claim coordination races.** Bead store's atomic claim is the
632	  only claim primitive. Two workers contending use the same store
633	  CAS that has worked correctly for as long as DDx has had a bead
634	  store.
635	- **No "kill server → workers exit" tests left valid.** Any test that
636	  relied on this behavior gets updated to assert "kill server →
637	  workers continue working autonomously."
638	- **Server restart loses derived view briefly.** The server's view of
639	  who's working what comes back within one probe cycle (default 30s)
640	  as workers re-register. No in-flight work is at risk.
641	- **Operator UX for cancel changes slightly.** Today operators kill
642	  worker subprocesses or wait. After: operators send cancel via the
643	  server (or write the marker to the bead store directly); worker
644	  honors at next safe point. Documented in CHANGELOG.
645	- **Evidence layout unchanged.** `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` still
646	  receives all per-attempt artifacts; worker writes there exactly as
647	  today.

246	// WakeProject signals every running execute-loop worker bound to projectRoot
247	// to skip its current idle-poll sleep and re-scan the ready queue. Used by
248	// the operator-prompt approve / auto-approve mutations (Story 15) so a
249	// freshly-approved bead is claimed immediately rather than after a full
250	// PollInterval. The send is non-blocking and the per-worker wake channel is
251	// buffered (cap 1), so multiple wakes within one tick coalesce. Returns the
252	// number of workers signalled (0 when no execute-loop is running for the
253	// project — submit/approve still succeeds, the next tick on a future worker
254	// will pick the bead up).
255	func (m *WorkerManager) WakeProject(projectRoot string) int {
256		m.mu.Lock()
257		defer m.mu.Unlock()
258		signalled := 0
259		for _, h := range m.workers {
260			if h == nil || h.wakeCh == nil {
261				continue
262			}
263			if h.record.ProjectRoot != projectRoot {
264				continue
265			}
266			select {
267			case h.wakeCh <- struct{}{}:
268				signalled++
269			default:
270				// Wake already pending — coalesce.
271				signalled++
272			}
273		}
274		return signalled
275	}

522		startedAt := time.Now().UTC()
523		phaseSeq := 1
524		sendProgress(progressCh, agent.ProgressEvent{

526			AttemptID: id,
527			WorkerID:  id,
528			ProjectID: spec.ProjectRoot,
529			Phase:     "running",
530			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
531			TS:        startedAt,
532			Message:   fmt.Sprintf("%s plugin %s", spec.Action, spec.Name),
533		})
534		if log != nil {
535			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(log, "%s plugin %s (%s)\n", spec.Action, spec.Name, spec.Scope)
536		}
537	
538		state, err := run(ctx)
539		if ctxErr := ctx.Err(); ctxErr != nil && err == nil {
540			err = ctxErr
541		}
542	
543		phase := "done"
544		message := state
545		if err != nil {
546			phase = "failed"
547			message = err.Error()
548		}
549		phaseSeq++
550		sendProgress(progressCh, agent.ProgressEvent{
551			EventID:   "evt-" + randomSuffix(8),
552			AttemptID: id,
553			WorkerID:  id,
554			ProjectID: spec.ProjectRoot,
555			Phase:     phase,
556			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
557			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
558			ElapsedMS: time.Since(startedAt).Milliseconds(),
559			Message:   message,
560		})
561	
562		if log != nil {
563			if err != nil {
564				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(log, "failed: %s\n", err)
565			} else {
566				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(log, "completed: %s\n", state)
567			}
568		}
569	
570		close(progressCh)
571		<-handle.progressDone
572	
573		m.mu.Lock()
574		record := handle.record
575		preservedState := ""
576		if record.State == "stopped" || record.State == "reaped" {
577			preservedState = record.State
578		}
579		record.FinishedAt = time.Now().UTC()
580		_ = handle.logFile.Close()
581		if err != nil {
582			record.State = "failed"
583			record.Status = "failed"
584			record.Error = err.Error()
585			record.LastError = err.Error()
586		} else {
587			record.State = "exited"
588			record.Status = "success"
589			record.LastResult = &WorkerExecutionResult{
590				AttemptID: id,
591				WorkerID:  id,
592				Status:    state,
593				Detail:    fmt.Sprintf("%s plugin %s", spec.Action, spec.Name),
594			}
595		}
596		if preservedState != "" {
597			record.State = preservedState
598			record.Status = preservedState
599		}
600		_ = m.writeRecord(dir, record)
601		handle.record = record
602		m.mu.Unlock()
603	}

605	// buildRoutePreflight constructs the route preflight gate (FEAT-006 D3, ddx-98e6e9ef).
606	// Before claiming a bead, the loop consults upstream ResolveRoute against the
607	// resolved (harness, model). If the upstream surfaces agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
608	// (or any other typed error), the loop exits with a worker-level execution_failed
609	// record — no bead is claimed, no executor invocation, no tier-attempt event burn.
610	// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only consumes the typed
611	// incompatibility surface.
612	//
613	// Returns nil (no preflight) when:
614	//   - hasBeadWorkerFactory is true (test injection path), OR
615	//   - spec.OpaquePassthrough is true (ddx work path — routing belongs to the agent
616	//     service), OR
617	//   - the operator pinned NOTHING (no Profile, no Model). In the auto-route case
618	//     the strict ResolveRoute path is too aggressive — it picks a default catalog
619	//     model from the harness's first entry and rejects exact-pin-only candidates.
620	//     Per ddx-9e4c238d / ddx-1e516bc9: bare auto-route must pass through to fizeau's
621	//     more lenient Execute path on each attempt.
622	func buildRoutePreflight(projectRoot string, spec ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, hasBeadWorkerFactory bool) func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
623		if hasBeadWorkerFactory || spec.OpaquePassthrough {
624			return nil
625		}
626		// Auto-route case: operator pinned no Profile and no Model. Skip strict
627		// preflight; let fizeau's Execute handle routing on each attempt.
628		if spec.Profile == "" && spec.Model == "" {
629			return nil
630		}
631		preflightSvc, preflightErr := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
632		return func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
633			svc := preflightSvc
634			if svc == nil {
635				return preflightErr
636			}
637			req := agentlib.RouteRequest{
638				Profile: agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(spec.Profile),
639			}
640			if harness != "" {
641				req.Harness = harness
642			}
643			if model != "" {
644				req.Model = model
645			}
646			_, rErr := svc.ResolveRoute(ctx, req)
647			return rErr
648		}
649	}

658	func (m *WorkerManager) runWorker(ctx context.Context, id, dir string, spec ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, projectRoot string, handle *workerHandle, log io.Writer, eventSink io.WriteCloser, progressCh chan agent.ProgressEvent) {
659		if eventSink != nil {
660			defer eventSink.Close() //nolint:errcheck
661		}
662		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
663	
664		var worker *agent.ExecuteBeadWorker
665		if m.BeadWorkerFactory != nil {
666			worker = m.BeadWorkerFactory(store)
667		} else {
668			// Build an executor that calls agent.ExecuteBead in-process, then
669			// submits the result to the project's land coordinator. The
670			// coordinator (a single goroutine per projectRoot) serializes all
671			// target-ref writes for this project — this is the server-side
672			// implementation of the human-PR landing model. See ddx-8746d8a6
673			// for the rationale. Prior to this rewrite, runWorker never called
674			// LandBeadResult at all, so commits produced by server-managed
675			// workers were silently lost (ddx-e14efc58).
676			coordinator := m.LandCoordinators.Get(projectRoot)
677	
678			// singleTierAttempt runs one execution at a specific harness/model.
679			singleTierAttempt := func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, tier escalation.ModelTier, resolvedHarness, resolvedProvider, resolvedModel string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
680				gitOps := &agent.RealGitOps{}
681				attemptProvider := spec.Provider
682				if resolvedProvider != "" {
683					attemptProvider = resolvedProvider
684				}
685				attemptRcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, config.CLIOverrides{
686					Harness:           resolvedHarness,
687					Model:             resolvedModel,
688					Provider:          attemptProvider,
689					ModelRef:          spec.ModelRef,
690					Effort:            spec.Effort,
691					OpaquePassthrough: spec.OpaquePassthrough,
692				})
693				beadStore := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
694				res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, attemptRcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
695					BeadEvents: beadStore,
696					BeadCancel: beadStore,
697				}, gitOps)
698				if err != nil && res == nil {
699					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, err
700				}
701				// Preserve operator-cancel results as-is; the worker has already
702				// classified them (preserved / operator_cancel / preserved_needs_review)
703				// and overriding here would lose the cancel signal.
704				operatorCancel := res != nil && res.Reason == agent.OperatorCancelReason
705				if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 && !operatorCancel {
706					if landErr := evaluateGatesAndSubmit(projectRoot, res, gitOps, coordinator, landSafetyConfigFromConfig(projectRoot), log); landErr != nil && err == nil {
707						err = landErr
708					}
709				} else if res != nil && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev && !operatorCancel {
710					res.Outcome = "no-changes"
711					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
712				} else if res != nil && res.ExitCode != 0 && !operatorCancel {
713					res.Outcome = "preserved"
714					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
715				}
716				// Safety net: commit any leftover evidence files that Land()
717				// did not pick up (e.g. HEAD was detached, or no land ran).
718				if res != nil && res.AttemptID != "" {
719					_ = agent.VerifyCleanWorktree(projectRoot, res.AttemptID)
720				}
721				if err != nil {
722					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, err
723				}
724				tierStr := ""
725				if tier != "" {
726					tierStr = string(tier)
727				}
728				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
729					BeadID:             res.BeadID,
730					AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
731					WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
732					Harness:            res.Harness,
733					Provider:           res.Provider,
734					Model:              res.Model,
735					ActualPower:        res.ActualPower,
736					Tier:               tierStr,
737					Status:             res.Status,
738					Detail:             res.Detail,
739					SessionID:          res.SessionID,
740					BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
741					ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
742					PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
743					NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
744					CostUSD:            res.CostUSD,
745					DurationMS:         int64(res.DurationMS),
746				}, nil
747			}

11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
25	--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
26	DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
27	within the requested power bounds.
28	
29	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
30	  drained     — no ready beads remain
31	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
32	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
33	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
34	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
35	
36	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
37	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
38	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
39	`,
40			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
41	  ddx work
42	
43	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
44	  ddx work --once
45	
46	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
47	  ddx work --poll-interval 30s
48	
49	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
50	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
51	
52	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
53	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
54			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
55			RunE: f.runWork,
56		}
57	
58		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
59		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
60		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
61		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
62		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
63		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
64		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
65		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
66		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
67		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
68		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
69		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 30*time.Second, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits (legacy opt-out). Default 30s keeps the worker alive across empty polls.")
70		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
71		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
72		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
73		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review")
74		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
76		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
77		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
78		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
79		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
80			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
81		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
82		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
83		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
84		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
85	
86		// Register "ddx work plan" as a subcommand of "ddx work".
87		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
88	
89		return cmd
90	}
91	
92	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
93	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
94	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
95	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
96	// by this layer.
97	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
98		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
99	}

Why this attempt declined to commit The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package. ### Actual surface (vs stated estimate) cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together: - in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction - per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization - in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` - in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation) - in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService) - per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net - progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` - watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS - terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result - BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via: - /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress - PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial) - worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging - Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent ### Test surface cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either: - a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or - deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface). This is the bulk of the work, not "test updates." ### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end) "server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit" Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper. ### Size-check verdict - More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables. - AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate). - More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes. input.content:
status: needs_investigation
reason: Bead scope materially exceeds the stated "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but `ddx bead create` invoked from this execution worktree produces malformed IDs prefixed with the worktree directory name (e.g. `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-...`). Decomposition must originate from the main repository (`~/Projects/ddx`) where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`. Operator triage required.

# What is done

Nothing. No source files modified. One malformed-ID child bead was created during exploration and closed (id `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T225413-91120fa0-5be5f460`) without affecting the bead store's open queue.

# Why this attempt declined to commit

The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package.

## Actual surface (vs stated estimate)

`cli/internal/server/workers.go` is 1,973 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 658-940, ~285 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together:

- in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction (lines 811-815)
- per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization (line 676; `m.LandCoordinators.Get(projectRoot)`)
- in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` (lines 801-809)
- in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (lines 753-781) — per-worker billed cost accumulation
- in-process `RoutePreflight` typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService (line 838 → `buildRoutePreflight` lines 622-649)
- per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net (lines 679-747)
- progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` (lines 421, 440, 1374)
- watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared `workerHandle.lastPhaseTS`
- terminal-state preservation (stopped / reaped) merged with loop result (lines 860-936)
- `BeadWorkerFactory` injection point for test fakes (workers.go:213, used by 7 sibling test files)

Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via:

- `/api/workers/{id}/event` ingestion (already exists from step 1, server.go:884) for progress
- PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists at workers.go:147; signal plumbing is partial in workers_reap_unix.go / workers_reap_windows.go)
- worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging
- Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent (currently the in-process wakeCh at workers.go:203 has no subprocess analogue)

## Test surface

`cli/internal/server/workers_test.go` alone is 1,107 lines. It and 6 sibling files use `BeadWorkerFactory` to inject a fake in-process executor:

- workers_test.go
- workers_stop_propagation_test.go
- workers_route_preflight_test.go
- workers_e2e_test.go
- workers_config_test.go
- graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go

With subprocess execution, `BeadWorkerFactory` is meaningless — the parent has no executor to inject; the child process owns it. Those tests need to be replaced by either:

- a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run=...&ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) that the parent execs in tests, or
- deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to `ddx work`'s own test surface in cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_*_test.go).

Plus 5 additional files use `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` directly:

- workers_watchdog_test.go
- workers_stop_test.go
- workers_prune_test.go
- graphql_backfill_test.go
- graphql_adapters.go (production code — adapter for GraphQL StartWorker mutation)

That's 12 files referencing structures that AC #3 demands be deleted. This is the bulk of the work, not "test updates."

## AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end)

> server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit

Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires:
- a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead (uses `ddx-50da9674` clean fixture, currently in flight)
- a stub or real claude binary on `$PATH` inside the test harness
- a server bound to a free port
- the new exec path wired in
- the worker registering against the running server's `/api/workers/register`
- an end-to-end commit landing through the `LandCoordinator` (which has moved out of the parent process into the child).

That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the existing `workers_e2e_test.go` suite — distinct from the refactor proper.

## Size-check verdict

Per the executor's "Step 0: size check":

- More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables.
- AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1-4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate).
- More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500-2,500 LOC of changes.

The bead's "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work. The bead label `triage:needs-investigation` is the bead author's own flag of this concern.

A previous attempt by claude on 2026-05-03T06:50 reached the same verdict (recorded as a long body event under `actor: erik` on the bead) but did not commit a `no_changes_rationale.txt` so the loop re-dispatched the bead.

# Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo)

Create the following children of `ddx-eccc6efb` from the **main repo** (`~/Projects/ddx`), not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree pick up the worktree dirname as the project prefix and are unusable.

## 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing

Title: `ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (parent-side plumbing)`

Scope:
- Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-940 with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via `os.Executable()` with `DDX_EXECUTABLE` env override for tests + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env).
- Record child PID on `WorkerRecord.PID`; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works (workers.go:1279, workers_reap_unix.go).
- Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by `POST /api/workers/{id}/event` being multiplexed back to the in-memory `progressCh` (preferred — endpoint already exists at server.go:884), or by tailing the child's `worker-events.jsonl`.
- Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess (mention in ADR-022 step 5 follow-up).
- Drop in-process `LandCoordinator` coupling, in-process `DefaultBeadReviewer`, in-process `CostCapTracker`, in-process `RoutePreflight` from runWorker (they now run inside the child).
- Keep `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` for now (compat with handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; deletion is 7b's scope).
- AC #5 end-to-end is NOT in 7a; 7a's tests cover (a) parent execs the child with the right argv+env, (b) child PID lands on WorkerRecord, (c) Stop signals the PID, (d) progress events from `/api/workers/{id}/event` reach SSE subscribers via drainProgress.

Estimate: 600-800 LOC including test rewrites of the 7 BeadWorkerFactory-using test files.

## 7b — handleStartExecuteLoopWorker exec wiring + delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec

Title: `ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → env+exec; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec`

Scope:
- Once 7a has landed and `runWorker` execs subprocess, refactor `handleStartExecuteLoopWorker` (server.go:2390-2461) to translate the HTTP request directly into env-vars + exec args without going through `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec`.
- Update `graphql_adapters.go:127` (StartWorker mutation adapter) to construct env+exec the same way.
- Delete `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` struct (workers.go:25-49) once nothing references it.
- Update remaining callers: workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_prune_test.go, graphql_backfill_test.go, graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go.
- Tests assert the new exec form (argv+env) and the absence of any JSON serialization of a parallel struct.

Depends on: 7a (runWorker must already exec; this bead is just the surface wiring above it).

Estimate: 150-300 LOC.

## 7c — End-to-end real-harness integration test

Title: `ADR-022 step 7c: e2e — server-spawn → exec ddx work → claude harness → bead commit`

Scope:
- Build a `cli/internal/server/workers_e2e_real_harness_test.go` that:
  1. Stands up a clean fixture repo via `ddx-50da9674`'s helper (or `testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, "standard")` — see CLAUDE.md "Test fixtures").
  2. Files one ready execution-eligible bead with a trivial change (e.g. add a TODO to a comment).
  3. Starts a real `ddx-server` bound to a free port.
  4. POSTs to `/api/agent/workers/execute-loop` with `--once` semantics; the server execs a subprocess via the new path (7a/7b).
  5. The subprocess registers against `/api/workers/register` (workerprobe at cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go).
  6. The subprocess invokes the claude harness — REAL claude binary if available on `$PATH`; otherwise a `scripted-claude-stub` that exercises the SAME exec args + env-var path the real claude harness uses (see cli/internal/agent/harness/claude.go for argv shape).
  7. The subprocess produces a commit on the bead's worktree, lands via the in-child LandCoordinator, and reports `result` event.
  8. The test asserts: registration succeeded with worker_id, the result event landed via /api/workers/{id}/event, and the bead's status transitioned to `done` with a non-empty `closing_commit`.
- Skip if claude binary unavailable AND scripted stub fixture not provided (mark as integration-only with build tag `e2e_real_harness`).

Depends on: 7a, 7b.

Estimate: 200-400 LOC for the test file + ~100 LOC for the scripted-claude-stub helper.

# Files to read for the implementation

- cli/internal/server/workers.go (the rewrite target)
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2390-2461 (handleStartExecuteLoopWorker)
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127 (other ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec caller)
- cli/cmd/work.go + cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1603-1900 (the runAgentExecuteLoopImpl that subprocess will exec)
- cli/internal/agent/workerprobe/probe.go (worker→server probe; already wired)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md (the contract)
- ADR-022 roadmap step 7 (line 601) — the source of this bead

# Recommendation

Operator: from `~/Projects/ddx` (main repo, not a worktree), run:

```
ddx bead create "ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (parent-side plumbing)" \
  --type task --priority 2 --parent ddx-eccc6efb \

ddx bead create "ADR-022 step 7c: e2e — server-spawn → exec ddx work → claude harness → bead commit" \
  --type task --priority 2 --parent ddx-eccc6efb \
  --labels "phase:2,area:server,kind:test,adr:022" \

ddx bead dep add <7b-id> <7a-id>
